name: Invesco DB Commodity Index Tracking Fund
business: The Fund seeks to track changes, whether positive or negative, in the level of the DBIQ Optimum Yield Diversified Commodity Index Excess Return TM (the Index) over time, plus the excess, if any, of the sum of the Fund's interest income from its holdings of United States Treasury Obligations, dividends from its holdings in money market mutual funds and dividends or distributions of capital gains from its holdings of T-Bill ETFs over the expenses of the Fund. The Fund invests in futures contracts in an attempt to track its Index. The Index is intended to reflect the economic performance of investing in futures contracts on a basket of commodities.
sellsTo: The Fund offers common units of beneficial interest (the Shares) only to certain eligible financial institutions (the Authorized Participants) in one or more blocks of 50,000 Shares (Creation Units).
namedSuppliers: The Bank of New York Mellon

exposures.sentence: Effective November 10, 2025, the Index comprised the following commodities: Light Sweet Crude Oil (WTI), Gas Oil, Ultra-Low Sulphur Diesel (also commonly known as Heating Oil), RBOB Gasoline, Natural Gas, Brent Crude, Gold, Silver, Platinum, Aluminum, Zinc, Comex Copper, Copper Grade A, Lead, Nickel, Feeder Cattle, Cocoa, Coffee, Corn, Cotton, Lean Hogs, Live Cattle, Wheat, Wheat (Kansas Wheat), Soybean Meal, Soybean Oil, Soybeans, and Sugar (each, an Index Commodity, and collectively, the Index Commodities).

catalysts.sentence: The Index is re-weighted on an annual basis on the sixth Index Business Day of each November, as discussed above.

Effective November 10, 2025, the Index comprised the following commodities: Light Sweet Crude Oil (WTI), Gas Oil, Ultra-Low Sulphur Diesel (also commonly known as Heating Oil), RBOB Gasoline, Natural Gas, Brent Crude, Gold, Silver, Platinum, Aluminum, Zinc, Comex Copper, Copper Grade A, Lead, Nickel, Feeder Cattle, Cocoa, Coffee, Corn, Cotton, Lean Hogs, Live Cattle, Wheat, Wheat (Kansas Wheat), Soybean Meal, Soybean Oil, Soybeans, and Sugar (each, an Index Commodity, and collectively, the Index Commodities).
Effective November 10, 2025, the Index methodology underwent a change.
